---
title: ""
---

# Beining Liu

Master's Student in Quantitative Economics  
New York University Stern School of Business

## Research Interests

- Economic History
- Political Economy
- Applied Microeconomics
- Culture and Informal Institutions

## About Me

I am a master's student in Quantitative Economics at NYU Stern. My research interests lie in economic history, political economy, and applied microeconomics. I am particularly interested in how historical events, informal institutions, and state-society interactions shape contemporary economic and political outcomes.

My current projects study topics including the Boxer Rebellion and modern nationalism in China, the Great Depression and entry into scientific careers, and literacy and political change in Tokugawa Japan.

## Current Research

### Boxer Rebellion Memory and State Response in Modern China

This project studies how local historical memory of the Boxer Rebellion affects contemporary nationalism, collective action, and state responses in China.

### The Great Depression and Entry into Scientific Careers

This project examines whether local exposure to the Great Depression affected the decision to enter scientific careers in the United States.

### Literacy, Information Diffusion, and the Meiji Restoration in Tokugawa Japan

This project studies whether the spread of terakoya education influenced political mobilization and modernization during the late Tokugawa period.

## Links

- [GitHub](https://github.com/BeiningLiu)
- [Google Scholar](#)
- [CV](cv.qmd)
- Email: liubeining [AT] nyu [DOT] edu
